DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jamie Martinez Esq, of Michael H. Kim, a Professional Law Corporation for PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT SANTIAGO FARIAS
Luis G. Molina, Esq. 415-647-6003 for Defendant/Cross-complainant Little Joe's Pizza.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court did not receive any contest email. Both sides argue. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT SANTIAGO FARIAS' DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

First (Breach of Contract) OVERRULED: Breach of an oral contract may be pled generally because it is rarely possible to plead the exact words. (Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) The allegations are sufficient to put Farias on notice of the issues to be admitted or denied.

Second (Conversion) SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND: "Money cannot be the subject of a cause of action for conversion unless there is a specific, identifiable sum involved." (McKell v. Washington Mut., Inc. (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 1457, 1491.)

Third (Fraud) OVERRULED: The cross-complaint is sufficiently specific.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2018 Continued To Apr-25-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
